title: Process operatives n.e.c.
description: Create or reproduce handmade objects for sale and exhibition using a variety of techniques, such as welding, weaving, pottery, and needlecraft. Lay and install carpet from rolls or blocks on floors. Install padding and trim flooring materials. Apply blocks, strips, or sheets of shock-absorbing, sound-deadening, or decorative coverings to floors. (Based on 55 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Depress pedals to rotate drums, wind specified numbers of plies around drums to form tire bodies, and collapse drums after processing is complete.
Inspect worn tires for faults, cracks, cuts, and nail holes, and determine if tires are suitable for retreading.
Place tires into molds for new tread and build semi-raw rubber treads onto buffed tire casings for vulcanization.
Measure and mark guidelines on surfaces or foundations, using chalk lines and dividers, to determine the best layout for installation.
Apply adhesive cement to floor or wall material to join and adhere foundation material, and roll and press sheet covering into cement base to smooth surface.
Lay out, position, and apply shock-absorbing, sound-deadening, or decorative coverings to floors, walls, and cabinets according to guidelines.
Inspect floors for smoothness, remove excess cement, and ensure the surface is firm and dry before installation.
Cut and trim carpet to fit along wall edges, openings, and projections, finishing edges with a wall trimmer, and stretch carpet over tack strips to align with walls.
Clean and paint completed tires, and trim excess rubber and imperfections during retreading processes as necessary.
Install carpet on floors using adhesive, ensuring to cut padding to size and remove old carpet and padding prior to installation.
Measure and cut materials according to blueprints, including flooring, tile, and adhesive materials, for efficient installation.
Remove and replace cracked or damaged tile, and apply filler compound and coats of finish to floors to seal wood.
Chip, scrape, or grind high spots, ridges, or rough projections to finish concrete using pneumatic chisels, power grinders, or hand tools.
Clean chipped areas using wire brushes and observe surface to determine if it is rough or uneven.
Build and clamp wooden molds around areas to be repaired using hand tools.
Signal truck driver to position truck for pouring concrete and move chute to direct concrete onto forms.
Wet concrete surfaces and rub with stone to smooth surfaces for the specified finish.
Mix cement, sand, and water to produce concrete, grout, or slurry using various hand tools or machines.
Observe temperature, humidity, and pressure gauges, adjusting controls to maintain prescribed operating conditions.
Apply hardening and sealing compounds to cure and restore surfaces of concrete.
Inspect products during and after processing to ensure they conform to specifications and quality standards.
Record operational data such as temperatures, processing times, or test results for quality control.
Move terrazzo installation materials, tools, or equipment to work areas, manually or using a wheelbarrow.
Wet surfaces to prepare for bonding, fill holes and cracks with grout or slurry, and smooth using a trowel.
Monitor conditions to detect effects of wind, heat, or cold on the curing process of concrete.
Produce a rough concrete surface using a broom.
Spread concrete and level it using tools such as rakes, shovels, or trowels to achieve a smooth finish.
Move controls to activate and adjust machines that extrude and form materials during processing.
Stop machines when specified amounts of products have been produced or if malfunctions are detected.
Notify other workers of defects and direct adjustments to machinery operations as needed.
Inspect machinery regularly to determine whether repairs are needed and notify maintenance accordingly.
Lay out patterns on wood stock based on blueprint specifications and use marking and measuring devices.
Repair or alter wooden furniture, cabinetry, and fixtures to maintain functionality and aesthetics.
Mark cutting lines or identifying information on stock, using marking pencils, rulers, or scribes.
Change or replace saw blades, cables, cutter heads, and grinding wheels, using hand tools.
Position stock along cutting lines, or against stops on beds of scoring or cutting machines.
Feed stock into cutting machines, onto conveyors, or under cutting blades, by threading, guiding, pushing, or turning handwheels.
Clean and lubricate cutting machines, conveyors, blades, saws, or knives, using steam hoses, scrapers, brushes, or oil cans.
Move stock or scrap to and from machines manually, or by using carts, handtrucks, or lift trucks.
Finish completed products or models with shellac, lacquer, wax, or paint.
Compute dimensions, areas, volumes, and weights for production specifications.
Inspect equipment or units to detect leaks or malfunctions, shutting equipment down, if necessary.
Correct patterns to compensate for defects in castings and ensure quality in final products.
Measure, weigh, and mix chemical ingredients or other materials according to specifications.
Review work orders, blueprints, specifications, or job samples to determine settings and adjustments for cutting machines.
Stack and sort processed materials for packaging, further processing, or shipping, according to types and sizes.
Set up, operate, or tend machines that cut or slice various materials, ensuring adherence to specifications.
Examine and verify dimensions or check the quality or fit of materials using measuring devices.
Attach parts or subassemblies together to form complete units, using various fastening methods.
Maintain pattern records, inventory supplies, and order necessary materials to ensure production flow.
Disassemble items to prepare them for finishing or repair, using appropriate hand tools.
Remove old finishes and damaged parts using hand tools and techniques to restore items.
Mock up and create patterns, templates, and jigs based on job specifications or customer needs.
Start machines and operational controls to feed and process materials while monitoring production standards.
Mix finishing ingredients to achieve desired colors or effects on products, applying them appropriately.
Clean and maintain equipment and tools using hand tools, solvents, brushes, and rags.
Apply coatings, such as paint or lacquer, to protect or decorate workpiece surfaces using spray guns, pens, or brushes.
Examine and measure completed materials or products to verify conformance to specifications using measuring devices.
Read job orders to determine production specifications, instructions, or required materials and inspect workpieces accordingly.
Compile operational or production data and maintain production logs, including recording gauge readings and test results.
Examine and inspect machines or equipment for hazards and malfunctions to ensure efficient and safe operation.
Monitor machine operations and observe gauges or panel lights to detect malfunctions and report or resolve any issues.
Dump, pour, or load specified amounts of materials into machinery or onto conveyors for further processing or storage.
Collect samples of materials or products for laboratory testing to ensure compliance with specified requirements.
Adjust machine components and set controls to regulate conditions such as material flow, temperature, and pressure according to specifications.
Monitor biofuels production processes and stored products until reused or transferred to users.
Preprocess feedstock for physical, chemical, or biological fuel production processes, ensuring compliance with safety regulations.
Weigh or measure materials at specified intervals to ensure conformance to production requirements.
Clean, adjust, and maintain biofuels processing equipment using hand tools, performing routine maintenance as needed.
Inspect ingredients or products, examining them visually or by hand to ensure compliance with established quality standards.
Record operational data from production, testing, and operations on specified forms.
Operate controls to start, stop, or adjust machinery that processes materials such as crushing or grinding.
Collect biofuels samples and conduct routine laboratory tests to assess product quality.
Inspect equipment for signs of wear and dislodge jammed materials from machinery using hand tools.
Reject defective products and adjust machinery to eliminate problems, ensuring operational efficiency.
Set up and operate machine tools to fabricate products based on blueprints and specifications.
Confer with engineering or manufacturing personnel to exchange technical information related to production.
Measure, examine, or test completed units for defects and compliance with specifications using precision instruments.
Clean workpieces or molds and lubricate machines as part of routine maintenance procedures.
Lay out, measure, and mark materials to prepare for cutting or assembly processes.
Instruct new workers in machinery operation and maintenance practices.
Repair mold defects, such as cracks or broken edges, using patterns, mold boxes, or hand tools.
Measure and cut products to specified dimensions, using measuring and cutting instruments, and verify dimensions with tools like calipers.
Smooth surfaces of molds and carvings using scraping tools, sandpaper, or rubbing stones.
Measure ingredients and mix molding or casting material to prescribed consistencies according to formulas, then remove excess materials from molds.
Position balls of clay on potters' wheels and operate the wheels, adjusting speeds as needed for shaping wares like vases and pitchers.
Prepare work for sale or exhibition, and maintain relationships with retail, pottery, art, and resource networks that facilitate sales or exhibitions.
Inspect, weigh, and measure products to verify conformance to specifications using instruments such as micrometers and calipers.
Operate kilns to fire pottery pieces, mix and apply glazes, and attach handles to pottery items.
Shape and finish stone by cutting, carving, and dressing surfaces according to diagrams or patterns, ensuring accuracy with measuring instruments.
Study blueprints and work orders to determine specifications, tooling instructions, and the sequences of operations for machine setups.
Clean work areas, maintain supplies of tools and materials, and order additional supplies as needed.
Compute unspecified dimensions and machine settings, using knowledge of metal properties and shop mathematics.
Install holding fixtures, cams, gears, and stops to control stock and tool movement, using hand tools, power tools, and measuring instruments.
Move toolholders manually or by turning handwheels, or engage automatic feeding mechanisms to feed tools to and along workpieces.
Turn valve handles to direct the flow of coolant onto work areas or to coat disks with spinning compounds.
Mount attachments, such as relieving or tracing attachments, to perform operations, such as duplicating contours of templates or trimming workpieces.
Observe milling or planing machine operations, adjust controls, and ensure conformance with specified tolerances.
Lift workpieces onto work tables either manually or with hoists, or direct crane operators to lift and position workpieces.
Test and adjust machine speeds or actions, according to product specifications, using gauges and hand tools.
Set up, operate, or tend machines to saw, cut, shear, slit, punch, crimp, notch, bend, or straighten metal or plastic materials.
Read work orders or production schedules to determine specifications, such as materials, cutting lines, or dimensions and tolerances.
Examine completed workpieces for defects, measure them to verify conformance to specifications, and sort defective pieces according to flaws.
Change worn cutting tools, using wrenches, and adjust and correct machine setups to reduce thicknesses and eliminate defects.
Position and secure workpieces on tables using bolts, jigs, clamps, or other holding devices.
Start machines, monitor their operations, record operational data, and observe operations to detect problems.
Install, align, and lock punches, dies, cutting blades, or other fixtures in machine beds or rams, using hand tools and templates.
Confer with customers to assess needs, obtain feedback, and develop concepts for crafted objects.
Create handmade objects using a variety of methods and materials, including cutting, shaping, fitting, and joining.
Develop product packaging, display, and pricing strategies, and advertise products using various media.
Clean work areas, machines, tooling, and parts using appropriate solvents or solutions.
Stack or load finished items, or place items on conveyor systems.
